purpose: Test whether student can build a complete equation from a variety-context visual using tiles. Tests full equation construction (Section 1 skill) with variety context (Section 2 skill) — combining two Lesson demands.
visual: Equal Groups with Pictures: 3 rows of 6 pencils on a desk. Pencils arranged in clear horizontal rows. Equation Builder with template `[___] × [___] = [___]`. Tile palette: `2`, `3`, 4, `5`, `6`, 9, 12, 15, 18, 20 .
student_action: Equation Builder tiles (Methods C/D — same as Lesson Section 1).
remediation_note: Direct student to count rows first (that's the number of groups = first factor), then count pencils in one row (items per group = second factor). Then skip-count by 6s three times.
design_note: Simplest problem — full equation with all numbers visible. Rows context confirms variety transfer — students saw rows of pencils in the equal vs. unequal groups task (2.2) but did not write an equation, so this is a natural extension. Uses non-fluency-family second factor (×6), consistent with Sections 1-2 principle: broader factors are appropriate when all numbers are given and the visual provides counting support. Values 3 × 6 = 18 not used in Lesson. Distractor tile `5` tests whether student counts rows accurately.
